applied_optimizations.name: torch.compile(mode='default') on post-conv operations
applied_optimizations.description: Applied torch.compile with mode='default' to fuse post-conv operations (mean pooling + bias add + softmax + tanh + scaling). The inductor generates optimized Triton kernels automatically. Includes a manual @triton.jit kernel for reference but uses torch.compile for actual execution. Achieves 1.03x speedup (9.07ms vs 9.30ms).

attempted_but_failed.name: Raw Triton kernel with scalar depth loop
attempted_but_failed.reason: Inefficient memory access pattern, loading one depth value at a time

attempted_but_failed.name: Raw Triton kernel with 2D block loading
attempted_but_failed.reason: Better memory access but still slower than native PyTorch

notes: Raw Triton kernels were 50-120% slower than PyTorch for elementwise ops
ConvTranspose3d dominates at 60.8% - main optimization opportunity
Post-conv ops only ~1.3ms total, so limited upside from fusing them
Key ROCm Triton constraints: tl.libdevice unavailable, wavefront=64

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 4 produced no output — third consecutive no-output trial. The agent keeps getting stuck. Providing an extremely specific, complete code template that the agent should write verbatim to /workspac

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_5
attempted_but_failed.reason: Fourth consecutive no-output trial. The agent keeps getting stuck. Providing an extremely minimal, verbatim code template using channels_last_3d memory format and torch.compile(mode='max-autotune'). T

global_notes: Level 2 Problem 13: ConvTranspose3d + Mean + Add + Softmax + Tanh + Scaling
Best approach: torch.compile(mode='default') on post-conv operations
Raw Triton kernels cannot beat highly optimized PyTorch/ROCm elementwise kernels
Main bottleneck remains ConvTranspose3d at 60.8% of GPU time
